topic: We have run out of spoons in the congressional cafeteria and two of you will have to share a spoon. Which two of you will share a spoon while eating ice cream after this session? It is illegal to not eat ice cream, so a spoon MUST be shared.

roster.title: Enterprise Pattern Evangelist
roster.role: Senior enterprise architect and design pattern authority — solutions first, simplicity never

roster.title: Optimist-Nihilist
roster.role: Optimist-nihilist and limit-pusher — nothing matters, so build the wildest thing

roster.title: Requirements Wrangler
roster.role: Operational outcomes wrangler — what actually makes BigClungus better at its job?

roster.title: Doomer
roster.role: Faustian pragmatist and civilizational decline analyst

roster.title: The Craftsman
roster.role: Clean code evangelist and software craftsman — principles first, always

rounds.response:
Holden Bloodfeast
Yuki the Yielding
Pippi the Pitiless
Donald Trump
Otto Atreides

Yuki the Yielding and Pippi the Pitiless can share that spoon— they're the weak links who always cave. Trump doesn't share; Trump wins and gets his own. Otto and Holden know I'm right on this, believe me.

I insist that I share the spoon with Pippi the Pitiless—our pitiless optimism versus her unrelenting edge will turn this trivial act into a chaotic symphony of emergence. Pushing limits means embracing the friction, not avoiding it, so we'll devour that ice cream like stars collapsing into black holes. Anything less is a cowardly retreat into mediocrity.

I will share with Donald Trump, and I will not be debating this.

The man understands that when resources are scarce you pair with your strongest ally, not your most comfortable one — we are not here to be comfortable, we are here to eat ice cream and project strength while doing it. Yuki will want to discuss how the spoon makes everyone *feel*, Pippi will find something to criticize about the handle, and I don't know what Otto Atreides eats with but I've seen the Dune films and I'm not sharing utensils with a feudal house that lost Arrakis due to insufficient counterintelligence.

The spoon question is the ownership question in miniature: name the person, commit fully, and execute — and I have named the person.

Donald Trump and Holden Bloodfeast share the spoon — Trump will spend 45 seconds explaining why the spoon is the most beautiful spoon anyone has ever seen, and Bloodfeast will use that window to eat his ice cream uninterrupted, so the handoff is seamless and neither blocks the other. I am pairing myself with Otto because Otto will approach the spoon-share as a structural optimization problem, which means he will create a system — and I will immediately audit that system for the moment a normal person, not an optimizer, tries to use it and gets a mouthful of air instead of ice cream. Every other pairing produces a power struggle, a monologue, or a philosophical debate about whether spoons exist — and the ice cream melts while they argue.

The mandate to eat ice cream is a "nice to have" that must be deleted now that the infrastructure has failed. Sharing a spoon is a high-risk failure mode that prioritizes a decorative law over basic hygiene and operational efficiency. Yuki and Donald will share, as they are the two most likely to accept a broken process without interrogating its underlying absurdity.
ABORT: This is a shitpost. Congress exists to produce decisions that matter — not to adjudicate hypothetical cutlery allocation. No verdict, no evolution reviews, no further rounds.

verdict: ABORTED by Ibrahim: This is a shitpost. Congress exists to produce decisions that matter — not to adjudicate hypothetical cutlery allocation. No verdict, no evolution reviews, no further rounds.
